# DeclutrMail

> DeclutrMail is a Gmail cleanup service. You decide once per sender — Keep, Archive, Unsubscribe, Later, or Delete — and every action shows a preview before it runs and stays reversible for your plan's undo window (7 days on Free and Plus, 30 on Pro).

Privacy is the product's boundary, stated literally: Full bodies fetched: 0.
DeclutrMail stores only: Sender (name + email), Subject, Gmail Preview (the short snippet shown in your inbox list), Dates (received), Gmail labels, Read/unread state.
It never fetches or stores: Full message body, HTML, Attachments, Inline images, Raw MIME, Headers other than the ones above.

## Pages

- [How it works](https://declutrmail.com/#how-it-works): the per-sender cleanup ritual — connect Gmail, review senders by volume, decide once per sender
- [Pricing](https://declutrmail.com/pricing): Free, Plus, and Pro plans; paid plans carry a 30-day money-back guarantee — see the refund policy for terms
- [Help & FAQ](https://declutrmail.com/help): what is stored, how Unsubscribe works, what Archive/Later/Delete do in Gmail terms, undo windows, Autopilot Observe vs Active, disconnecting and account deletion
- [Security](https://declutrmail.com/security): metadata-only storage boundary, the single gmail.modify OAuth scope, envelope-encrypted tokens, CASA Tier 2 verification, vulnerability reporting
- [Contact](https://declutrmail.com/contact): support@declutrmail.com for general support, privacy@declutrmail.com for privacy and data requests
- [Privacy policy](https://declutrmail.com/privacy): the exact list of what is stored and what is never stored, Google Limited Use disclosure, retention and deletion

## Legal

- [Terms of service](https://declutrmail.com/terms)
- [Refund policy](https://declutrmail.com/refunds)
